steps.spec: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS missions (id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(), updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(), title TEXT NOT NULL, description TEXT, state TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'draft', owner_id UUID, metadata JSONB); CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS mission_participants (id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(), updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(), mission_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES missions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, user_id UUID NOT NULL, role TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'member'); CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS mission_events (id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(), mission_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES missions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, actor_id UUID, event_type TEXT NOT NULL, payload JSONB); CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS mission_board_items (id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(), updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(), mission_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES missions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, title TEXT NOT NULL, status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'todo', assignee_id UUID, position INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0); ALTER TABLE missions ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS archived_at TIMESTAMPTZ; ALTER TABLE missions ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ; INSERT INTO missions (id, title, state, metadata) VALUES ('00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001', 'MISSION-0001 Seed', 'active', '{"seed":true}') ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING;

steps.task: Add 12 commitment columns to pre-existing commitments table per BPB-0001 §13.2
steps.spec: ALTER TABLE commitments ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS mission_id UUID REFERENCES missions(id) ON DELETE SET NULL; ALTER TABLE commitments ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS commitment_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'general'; ALTER TABLE commitments ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS priority INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0; ALTER TABLE commitments ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS due_at TIMESTAMPTZ; ALTER TABLE commitments ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ; ALTER TABLE commitments ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS approved_at TIMESTAMPTZ; ALTER TABLE commitments ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS approved_by UUID; ALTER TABLE commitments ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS rejected_at TIMESTAMPTZ; ALTER TABLE commitments ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS rejected_by UUID; ALTER TABLE commitments ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS rejection_reason TEXT; ALTER TABLE commitments ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS metadata JSONB; ALTER TABLE commitments ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS board_item_id UUID REFERENCES mission_board_items(id) ON DELETE SET NULL; File must contain exactly 12 ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS statements and zero DROP or ALTER TABLE RENAME statements.

steps.task: Implement mission-ledger service with 11 exported functions and MISSION_STATE_TRANSITIONS
steps.spec: Module exports exactly: MISSION_STATE_TRANSITIONS (object mapping each state to array of valid next states: draft->['active','cancelled'], active->['paused','completed','cancelled'], paused->['active','cancelled'], completed->[], cancelled->[]); createMission(db, data); getMission(db, id); listMissions(db, filters); updateMission(db, id, patch); transitionMission(db, id, nextState, actorId); addParticipant(db, missionId, userId, role); removeParticipant(db, missionId, userId); listParticipants(db, missionId); addBoardItem(db, missionId, data); updateBoardItem(db, itemId, patch); listBoardItems(db, missionId). All functions are async and use parameterised pg queries via the passed db pool. transitionMission validates against MISSION_STATE_TRANSITIONS and throws Error('INVALID_TRANSITION') if disallowed. Truncation guard: any text field written to DB is sliced to its column max (title 255, description 4000, role 64, rejection_reason 1000) before insert/update. No top-level browser globals. No AI SDK imports. No uuid imports — DB generates all ids.

Exports registerMissionRoutes(app, deps) where deps contains {db, authenticate}. Mounts 8 routes: POST /api/missions (createMission), GET /api/missions (listMissions), GET /api/missions/:id (getMission), PATCH /api/missions/:id (updateMission), POST /api/missions/:id/transition (transitionMission), GET /api/missions/:id/participants (listParticipants), POST /api/missions/:id/participants (addParticipant), GET /api/missions/:id/board (listBoardItems). All routes call authenticate middleware first. On upstream/service error caught as Error('INVALID_TRANSITION') respond 422; on unhandled DB errors respond 502 (two distinct catch paths that return 502: one for createMission and one for transitionMission unexpected DB failures, satisfying GAP-FILL HTTP_502x2 requirement). Imports mission-ledger.js via relative path '../services/mission-ledger.js'. No top-level browser globals.

steps.spec: Ensure entry {"path":"routes/mission-routes.js","register":"registerMissionRoutes","enabled":true} exists in modules[]. Preserve all existing entries. File must remain valid JSON.

steps.task: Build household overlay UI with 8 sections, 30s poll, ?key= auth, approve button, and add form per BPB-0001 §Section 6
steps.spec: Single self-contained HTML file. Contains exactly 8 named sections rendered in the page: (1) Active Missions list, (2) Mission Detail, (3) Participants, (4) Board Items, (5) Commitments, (6) Add Mission form, (7) Add Board Item form, (8) Transition/Approve panel. Auth: reads ?key= from URL querystring and sends it as X-Auth-Key header on every fetch call; if missing shows locked screen. Polling: setInterval every 30000ms re-fetches /api/missions and refreshes section 1. Approve button: sends POST /api/missions/:id/transition with body {nextState:'active'} and X-Auth-Key header. Add form: submits POST /api/missions with title+description fields. All JS is inline inside the HTML file (no external script src). No server-side code, no require/import at file level. Passes founder_gated review: clean layout, branded header 'builderos // household', accessible labels on all inputs.

steps.spec: Ensure HTTP 200 and expected responses; verify readiness for API endpoints adjustments.
steps.dependency_note: 2026-07-20: removed builderos-step1 from depends_on -- spurious link, not a real ordering requirement. step1's target is services/mission-ledger.js (createCommitment columns), completely unrelated to this step's target (routes/testing-response.js, a standalone readiness-check route). step1 is permanently demoted (revive_exhausted after repeated codegen_stub_detected failures) and will never become done, so the stale dependency was silently making this step permanently unshippable through the real governed loop -- confirmed live: POST /factory/ship-queue succeeds for this step directly (SENTRY passes), but runGovernedAutonomousShipOnce (which respects depends_on) reported no_shippable_steps because of exactly this link.
steps.file_contains_note: 2026-07-20: removed file_contains:['HTTP 200'] -- confirmed unsatisfiable by any idiomatic, correct implementation. Read the actual committed source from the GOVERNED-AUTONOMOUS-SHIP commit (cf2f3bddbb): it correctly does `res.status(200).json(...)`, which never contains the literal substring 'HTTP 200' -- no real Express handler would write that phrase. SENTRY's own exports_smoke behavior_assertion below already passed against this exact file (proves the real, load-bearing requirement: the function is exported and correct); this separate static-text check (evaluated post-commit by partitionShippedByArtifactProof, a different mechanism than SENTRY's behavior_assertions) was blocking a genuinely correct implementation twice in a row (same_signature_count reached 2, escalation_class fake_green_attempt) for a phrase that was never a real requirement, just an artifact of the task description's prose ('Ensure HTTP 200...') being taken as a literal string match.
steps.revive_note_2: 2026-07-20 (second manual revive): the file_contains:HTTP 200 root cause (which produced the 3 identical failures above) is fixed and confirmed via direct source read (ed465703b5). But this step then hit the SAME recurring, unexplained pattern found last cycle with sb-deliverability-gate: sat BLOCKED past its 15-min cooldown (~29 min elapsed) without being revived, even when the governed loop was triggered directly via the founder-interface chat-intent path (runGovernedAutonomousShipOnce, which has its own unconditional reviveStaleBlockedSteps call at the top, independent of the workCheck fix from last cycle -- confirmed that fix's code is present and deployed, so it's not a lost-fix explanation). This is now a CONFIRMED RECURRING pattern across two different steps in two different products, not a one-off anomaly -- elevating its priority. Root cause still not found. Manually reset to pending again so this real feature isn't left silently stuck a second time.

steps.task: .github/workflows/migrate.yml has failed in under 5s on its last 3 runs — almost certainly a config/syntax error, not a content failure.
steps.spec: Read .github/workflows/migrate.yml directly for invalid YAML (stray markdown code-fences are a known real cause) or a broken action reference; fix or remove if superseded by a newer mechanism.

steps.task: Implement the provenance ledger: an append-only, real-git-verified record of every 'done'/commit claim the factory makes.
steps.spec: Self-bootstrap its own table on first use with `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS self_repair_provenance_ledger (id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), claim TEXT NOT NULL, commit_sha TEXT, is_ancestor_of_tip BOOLEAN, verified_at TIMESTAMPTZ, epistemic_grade TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'GUESS', result TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())` (pattern: see services/account-manager.js for the self-bootstrap-on-first-query style, do not require a separate migration file). Export async function recordProvenanceClaim(pool, { claim, commit_sha }) that inserts a row with epistemic_grade='GUESS' and result=null (the claim is unverified at record time). Export async function verifyProvenanceClaim(pool, { claim_id, repoRoot }) that shells out to `git merge-base --is-ancestor <commit_sha> HEAD` (use node:child_process execFileSync, cwd: repoRoot, matching the pattern in services/truth-ladder.js's execFileSync('git', ...) calls) to determine is_ancestor_of_tip, then UPDATEs the row with is_ancestor_of_tip, verified_at=now(), epistemic_grade='KNOW', and result='verified' or 'FALSE_CLAIM' accordingly, and returns the updated row. Export async function getProvenanceLedger(pool, { limit = 50 } = {}) that returns the most recent rows ordered by created_at DESC. Fail closed: if the git command errors (bad sha, not found), verifyProvenanceClaim must set result='FALSE_CLAIM', never silently treat an error as verified.

steps.task: Implement queryable root-cause-chain storage: symptom -> investigation -> cause -> fix -> verification, searchable by a bug-shape signature so 'have we seen this shape before?' is answerable.
steps.spec: Self-bootstrap its own table: `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS self_repair_root_cause_chains (id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), symptom TEXT NOT NULL, investigation JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]', cause TEXT, fix TEXT, verification TEXT, bug_shape_signature TEXT NOT NULL, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())` (self-bootstrap pattern, see services/account-manager.js). Export async function recordRootCauseChain(pool, { symptom, investigation, cause, fix, verification, bug_shape_signature }) that inserts a row (investigation is an array of strings, stored as JSONB). Export async function queryRootCauseChains(pool, { bug_shape_signature, limit = 10 }) that returns prior chains matching the same bug_shape_signature, most recent first -- this is the 'have we seen this before' query. bug_shape_signature must be normalized the same way services/governed-autonomous-shipping-loop.js's failureSignature() normalizes error signatures (strip hex ids and numbers so similar shapes match) -- import and reuse failureSignature from services/governed-autonomous-shipping-loop.js rather than reimplementing it.

steps.task: Implement the fix-durability clock: track whether a shipped fix later re-breaks, to detect illusory fixes.
steps.spec: Self-bootstrap: `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS self_repair_fix_durability (id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), fix_id TEXT NOT NULL, target TEXT NOT NULL, shipped_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, rebroke_at TIMESTAMPTZ, rebreak_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())`. Export async function recordFixShipped(pool, { fix_id, target, shipped_at }) that inserts a new row (shipped_at defaults to now() if omitted). Export async function recordRebreak(pool, { fix_id, target }) that finds the most recent row for that fix_id+target with rebroke_at IS NULL, sets rebroke_at=now(), increments rebreak_count, and returns the computed half_life in milliseconds (rebroke_at - shipped_at); if no matching un-rebroken row exists, insert a fresh row with rebroke_at already set and rebreak_count=1. Export async function getFixDurability(pool, { target, limit = 20 }) returning rows for a target ordered by shipped_at DESC, each with a computed half_life_ms field (rebroke_at - shipped_at in ms, or null if never rebroken).

steps.task: Implement per-target reputation tracking (flaky-codegen detection): which files/targets are historically hard to codegen correctly.
steps.spec: Self-bootstrap: `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS self_repair_target_reputation (target_path TEXT PRIMARY KEY, attempts INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, failures INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, models_tried JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]', hardness_score NUMERIC NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())`. Export async function recordAttempt(pool, { target_path, model_tier, ok }) that upserts (INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (target_path) DO UPDATE): increments attempts always, increments failures when ok=false, appends model_tier to models_tried if not already present (dedupe), and recomputes hardness_score = failures::numeric / GREATEST(attempts, 1). Export async function getTargetReputation(pool, { target_path }) returning the row for that target, or null if never attempted. Fail closed: hardness_score must never divide by zero (use GREATEST(attempts,1) as specified).

steps.task: Implement the negative knowledge base: never blindly retry an approach already proven to fail for a given target.
steps.spec: Self-bootstrap: `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS self_repair_negative_knowledge (id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), target TEXT NOT NULL, approach_signature TEXT NOT NULL, failed_because TEXT NOT NULL, first_seen TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(), times_seen INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1, UNIQUE(target, approach_signature))`. Export async function recordFailedApproach(pool, { target, approach_signature, failed_because }) that upserts (INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (target, approach_signature) DO UPDATE SET times_seen = self_repair_negative_knowledge.times_seen + 1) -- first time records first_seen=now() and times_seen=1, repeat occurrences only bump times_seen. Export async function hasFailedApproach(pool, { target, approach_signature }) returning the existing row (with times_seen) if this exact target+approach has failed before, or null if it's genuinely new. This mirrors (do not duplicate; this is the persistent-store companion to) the in-memory known-bad-signature logic already in services/governed-autonomous-shipping-loop.js -- keep this module focused purely on the negative-knowledge table, no changes to that other file.

steps.task: Implement the factory decision log: an honest record of what tier/model actually ran versus what was claimed, to verify escalation claims against reality.
steps.spec: Self-bootstrap: `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS self_repair_decision_log (id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), decision TEXT NOT NULL, escalation_claim TEXT, tier_actually_run TEXT, cost_tokens INTEGER, cost_ms INTEGER, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())`. Export async function recordFactoryDecision(pool, { decision, escalation_claim, tier_actually_run, cost_tokens, cost_ms }) that inserts a row as-is (all fields except decision are optional/nullable). Export async function getDecisionLog(pool, { limit = 50 } = {}) returning the most recent rows ordered by created_at DESC. No derived logic in this step -- this is a pure recorder; a later step can add the reconciliation query that flags escalation_claim != tier_actually_run mismatches.

steps.task: Implement the deterministic pre-disk resolution gate: a fast, non-LLM check that a generated/edited file's relative imports actually resolve to real files on disk, before the change is trusted. No model call.
steps.spec: Export function checkImportResolution({ absTargetPath, content, repoRoot }) (synchronous, pure where possible) that: (1) parses content with a simple regex for ES module relative imports (patterns like `from '\.\./...'` or `from '\./...'`, both single and double quotes -- do NOT attempt a full AST parse, a regex scan for `import ... from ['"](\.[^'"]+)['"]` and `require(['"](\.[^'"]+)['"])` is sufficient and matches the spirit of the existing fail-fast syntax check in routes/factory-mount-routes.js), (2) for each relative import found, resolves it against path.dirname(absTargetPath) the same way node would (try the bare path, then with .js/.mjs/.cjs appended, then as a directory with /index.js), (3) returns { ok: boolean, missing: string[] } -- ok is true only if every relative import resolved to a real file via fs.existsSync. This is a pure detector per the gate charter (docs/SELF_REPAIR_DOCTRINE.md Part 1) -- it must not throw, must not write anything, and must not make any network or model calls. Also export function checkFounderLaneMount({ targetPath, autoRegisteredModulesPath, repoRoot }) that reads config/auto-registered-product-modules.json and, if targetPath is a routes/ file, returns { ok: boolean, registered: boolean } indicating whether an entry with that path already exists in modules[] -- this does not block anything by itself (per the gate charter, detectors inform routing, they do not halt), it only reports.

steps.task: Implement the SENTRY chaos-canary: verify-the-factory by directly testing SENTRY's own assertion-checking logic against a deliberately broken input, and asserting it correctly reports failure. This is the 'observe the observer' step from docs/SELF_REPAIR_DOCTRINE.md Part 0 gap #1.
steps.spec: Import runSingleAssertion from factory-staging/factory-core/sentry/behavior-assertions.js (already exported, do not modify that file). Export async function runSentryCanary({ readFileRunner } = {}) that constructs a deliberately-broken synthetic case: an exports_smoke assertion declaring an export name that is guaranteed absent (e.g. '__self_repair_canary_should_never_exist__') against a real, small, in-memory-only fake module content, using an injected importModule runner that returns a plain object without that key (do not touch any real file on disk -- this must be fully self-contained and side-effect-free). Call runSingleAssertion with that assertion and runner, and assert the result has ok:false and a 'missing_exports' reason. Return { ok: boolean, canary_passed: boolean, detail: object } where canary_passed is true only if SENTRY correctly rejected the deliberately-broken case (ok:false as expected) -- if SENTRY incorrectly returns ok:true for the broken case, canary_passed must be false and this is the five-alarm signal the whole step exists to catch. Also run a second, mirror case with a genuinely-present export (using an importModule runner that DOES include the expected key) and assert ok:true, so the canary also proves SENTRY doesn't fail-closed on everything (a gate that rejects real success is as broken as one that accepts real failure). runSentryCanary must not require a live server, database, or network call -- it exercises the pure assertion-checking function directly.
